selection_note: Representative direct Noble dependents chosen to span library integration and executable applications. Phase 10 extends the matrix to 15 binary packages: 3 library-flavored compile probes plus 12 executable scenarios backed by committed fixtures and pinned package/image metadata.

dependents.name: APT / libapt-pkg
dependents.runtime_functionality: libapt-pkg uses libgcrypt as its hashing backend when computing and checking digests for package indexes and package files, which feeds source authentication and retrieved-data validation.
runtime_evidence: apt-pkg/contrib/hashes.cc initializes libgcrypt, enables requested hash algorithms with gcry_md_enable, streams file contents with gcry_md_write, and returns digests with gcry_md_read.
The libapt-pkg package description says the runtime library authenticates sources and validates retrieved data.

dependents.name: GNU Privacy Guard
dependents.runtime_functionality: OpenPGP payload decryption, integrity checking, signature handling, and public-key operations rely on libgcrypt primitives.
runtime_evidence: g10/decrypt-data.c uses gcry_cipher_* and gcry_md_* for encrypted-data packet handling, AEAD/MDC processing, and message integrity verification.
g10/pkglue.c uses gcry_pk_encrypt, gcry_pk_verify, gcry_sexp_* and gcry_mpi_* helpers for public-key encryption and signature verification paths.

dependents.name: GNOME Keyring
dependents.runtime_functionality: Stored keyring secrets are encrypted and decrypted with libgcrypt, and password-derived keys and IVs are generated from libgcrypt-backed KDF and hash code.
runtime_evidence: pkcs11/secret-store/gkm-secret-binary.c derives an AES-128 key and IV from the master password with egg_symkey_generate_simple(... GCRY_CIPHER_AES128, GCRY_MD_SHA256 ...) and then calls gcry_cipher_encrypt and gcry_cipher_decrypt on the keyring buffer.
egg/egg-symkey.c uses libgcrypt hash, HMAC, MPI, and cipher APIs to implement the key-derivation and cipher-setup helpers used by the keyring backends.

dependents.name: libssh gcrypt flavor
dependents.runtime_functionality: SSH session crypto in the gcrypt backend uses libgcrypt for HMACs, block ciphers, AEAD modes, big-integer helpers, and related key material handling.
runtime_evidence: The libssh-gcrypt-4 package description explicitly says it is the gcrypt-linked libssh flavor.
src/libgcrypt.c maps SSH HMACs to gcry_md_* calls and sets up Blowfish, AES-CBC, AES-CTR, AES-GCM, and other packet-protection primitives with gcry_cipher_*.

dependents.name: xmlsec1 gcrypt backend
dependents.runtime_functionality: This package is xmlsec's libgcrypt-backed crypto engine for XML Signature and related XML security transforms.
runtime_evidence: The package description says it provides the gcrypt implementation of the XML security library cryptography functions.
src/gcrypt/signatures.c opens libgcrypt digest contexts with gcry_md_open and wires libgcrypt-backed RSA signature and verification transforms into xmlsec.

dependents.name: MUNGE
dependents.runtime_functionality: Credential creation and validation use libgcrypt for the service's symmetric encryption, digests, and HMAC-based authentication.
runtime_evidence: The package description says MUNGE credentials are defined by and validated with a shared cryptographic key.
src/munged/cipher.c uses gcry_cipher_open, gcry_cipher_setkey, gcry_cipher_setiv, and gcry_cipher_encrypt/decrypt, while src/common/mac.c and src/common/md.c use libgcrypt HMAC and digest APIs.

dependents.name: Aircrack-ng

dependency_evidence.compile_time: aircrack-ng 1:1.7+git20230807.4bf83f1a-1build2 Build-Depends includes libgcrypt-dev | libgcrypt20-dev | libgcrypt11-dev

dependents.runtime_functionality: The WEP/WPA cracking suite uses libgcrypt for most of its packet and key-cracking crypto helpers, including AES, ARCFOUR, MD5, SHA-256, and AES-CMAC code paths.
runtime_evidence: debian/README.Debian says Debian's build uses an internal SHA1 implementation and relies on libgcrypt for the rest of the crypto code.
lib/crypto/aes-128-cbc-gcrypt.c, arcfour-gcrypt.c, md5-gcrypt.c, sha256-gcrypt.c, and mac-omac1-gcrypt.c are libgcrypt-backed implementations used by the suite.

dependents.name: Wireshark
dependents.runtime_functionality: Wireshark uses libgcrypt for encrypted-traffic analysis, especially 802.11/WPA and TDLS key derivation, packet decryption, and MIC verification, plus some capture hashing helpers.
runtime_evidence: epan/crypt/dot11decrypt.c, dot11decrypt_ccmp.c, and dot11decrypt_gcmp.c use gcry_md_*, gcry_mac_*, and gcry_cipher_* to derive wireless keys, verify MICs, and decrypt CCMP/GCMP traffic.
editcap.c and capinfos.c also use libgcrypt digest APIs for packet and capture hashing tasks.

dependents.name: GnuPG signature verifier
dependents.runtime_functionality: Detached OpenPGP signature verification uses libgcrypt digest and public-key verification primitives through the GnuPG verification path.
runtime_evidence: gpgv is the GnuPG signature verification binary and links directly to libgcrypt20 in Noble.
The committed scenario verifies a detached signature using a committed trusted keyring and asserts gpgv loads the selected libgcrypt.so.20.

dependents.name: GnuPG S/MIME
dependents.runtime_functionality: S/MIME signing, verification, encryption, and decryption use libgcrypt public-key, digest, and cipher primitives underneath GnuPG's X.509 implementation.
runtime_evidence: gpgsm is the GnuPG S/MIME binary and links directly to libgcrypt20 in Noble.
The committed scenario imports committed X.509 material, signs, verifies, encrypts, decrypts, and checks gpgsm loads the selected libgcrypt.so.20.

dependents.name: SECCURE
dependents.runtime_functionality: The seccure tool suite uses libgcrypt for ECC key derivation, ECIES-style encryption, ECDSA-style signatures, digests, and AES/HMAC operations.
runtime_evidence: seccure-key, seccure-encrypt, seccure-decrypt, seccure-sign, and seccure-verify link to libgcrypt20 in Noble.
The committed scenario runs those five commands using committed passphrase and message fixtures.

dependents.name: pdfgrep
dependents.runtime_functionality: Searching password-protected PDFs exercises pdfgrep's libgcrypt-linked password and encrypted-document handling path.
runtime_evidence: The Noble pdfgrep binary links directly to libgcrypt20.
The committed scenario searches a committed password-protected PDF with pdfgrep --password and asserts the selected libgcrypt.so.20 is loaded.

dependents.name: rng-tools5
dependents.runtime_functionality: The package's rngd binary links to libgcrypt while rngtest validates FIPS statistics over supplied entropy samples.
runtime_evidence: rng-tools5 ships rngd and rngtest; rngd links directly to libgcrypt20 in Noble.
The committed scenario asserts rngd loads the selected libgcrypt.so.20 and runs rngtest -c against a committed known-good random fixture.

dependents.name: Off-the-Record toolkit
dependents.runtime_functionality: The OTR toolkit uses libgcrypt-backed hash, MAC, AES, and Diffie-Hellman helpers for message key derivation and transcript tooling.
runtime_evidence: libotr5-bin toolkit commands link directly to libgcrypt20 in Noble.
The committed scenario runs otr_mackey and otr_sesskeys against committed derivation vectors.

dependents.name: tcplay
dependents.runtime_functionality: tcplay uses libgcrypt for TrueCrypt-compatible PBKDF and cipher metadata handling when reading encrypted volume headers.
runtime_evidence: The Noble tcplay binary links directly to libgcrypt20.
The committed scenario runs tcplay --info against a committed small volume fixture and validates PBKDF and cipher metadata without mapping the volume.
